pi_items.source: HTTP HEAD request confirmed HTTP 200 status; dataset successfully downloaded and validated
CDC WISQARS and public health standards confirm 75 years as standard reference age for US YPLL calculations (https://wisqars.cdc.gov/help/leading-causes-of-death-and-years-of-potential-life-lost/)
Parameter specification in task.json metadata; tolerance value for verification logic
Calculated from actual Titanic dataset: 424 passengers with Survived=0 and known Age; YPLL = max(75-Age, 0) for each; sum = 18814.5. Dataset verified from GitHub datasciencedojo/datasets repository.
Counted from Titanic CSV: total 549 passengers with Survived=0, but only 424 have non-null Age values
Line count of Titanic CSV file (891 total passenger records)
Maximum age in filtered dataset (Survived=0, Age known); no deceased passengers were aged 75+

privileged_info_summary: The Titanic dataset is real historical data from the 1912 disaster. When filtering for deceased passengers (Survived=0) with known age (n=424) and calculating YPLL using reference age 75 (YPLL = max(75-Age, 0)), the correct sum is 18814.5. The verifier's hardcoded ground truth value of 18235.0 contradicts the actual calculated value by 579.5 years (over 10 times the stated tolerance of 50). The metadata-specified reference age of 75 years is verified as correct per CDC public health standards.
